topic: CO₂ volume vs CaCO₃ mass
image_prompt: SVG: scatter graph, x-axis Mass of CaCO₃ / g (1.00 to 3.00, major ticks every 0.50, minor every 0.10), y-axis Volume of CO₂ produced / cm³ (200 to 700, ticks every 100, minor every 20). ~17 points rising near-linearly from (1.4,313) to (3.0,670) with anomalies at 1.9 g (~540, high) and 2.5 g (~480, low).

topic: CO₂ volume vs time (limestone vs oyster)

render_data.title: Volume of CO₂ produced vs time — limestone vs crushed oyster shell
render_data.note: Average rate = total volume of gas ÷ total time. Read the total volume at t = 60 s and divide by 60: limestone ≈ 1.33 cm³ s⁻¹, crushed oyster shell ≈ 0.28 cm³ s⁻¹.

topic: Time in digestive system bar charts
image_prompt: SVG: two bar-chart panels. Panel 'All data' (y-axis Time in digestive system / h, 0–12) grouped bars per Trial one/two/three — Limestone (teal) 5,3,6 and Crushed oyster shell (orange) 11.1,10.6,11.1, with key. Panel 'Limestone graph' (y-axis 0–8) Limestone bars 5,3,6.

topic: Oyster shell digestion-time bar chart
image_prompt: SVG: single bar chart 'Crushed oyster shell graph', y-axis Time in digestive system / h (0–12), Crushed oyster shell (orange) bars for Trial one/two/three at 11.1, 10.6, 11.1, with key.

topic: Solubility of Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ salts (data table)
image_prompt: SVG data table: 'Solubility in water at 25 °C of Ca²⁺ and Mg²⁺ ions'. Columns Anion / Mg²⁺ / Ca²⁺. Rows: Cl⁻,Br⁻,I⁻ = soluble, soluble; SO₄²⁻ = soluble, insoluble; OH⁻ = insoluble, insoluble; CO₃²⁻,PO₄³⁻ = insoluble, insoluble. Teal shaded header, ruled grid.

topic: Percentage by mass of calcium carbonate (data table)
image_prompt: SVG data table: Animal vs 'Percentage by mass of calcium carbonate / %' split into Teeth and Bone. Rows: Elephant 20.93/22.58; Horse 28.80/24.70; Human 29.82/23.10; Monkey 29.93/23.12; Dolphin 22.12/15.16. Teal header, ruled grid.

topic: Gas syringe + conical flask apparatus
image_prompt: SVG apparatus: horizontal gas syringe (graduated barrel + plunger) connected by delivery tubing through a bung into a conical flask containing a reaction mixture with effervescence and solid chips. Labels: Gas syringe, Conical flask, Reaction mixture.

topic: Percentage by mass of calcium carbonate (data table, repeated in Q4)
image_prompt: SVG data table: Animal vs 'Percentage by mass of calcium carbonate / %' split into Teeth and Bone. Rows: Elephant 20.93/22.58; Horse 28.80/24.70; Human 29.82/23.10; Monkey 29.93/23.12; Dolphin 22.12/15.16. Teal header, ruled grid.

topic: Limestone vs crushed oyster shell particle size
image_prompt: SVG diagram comparing dietary supplements: left a single large limestone chip (~7 mm diameter) magnified in a callout with a pile beneath, labelled Limestone; right a fine powder of crushed oyster shell (~0.5 mm particles) magnified with a powder pile, labelled Crushed oyster shell. Note '*approximate diameter'.

topic: Hazard symbols (corrosive, flammable, toxic)
image_prompt: SVG: three reagent bottles each bearing a red-bordered GHS hazard diamond. Image 1 = corrosive (acid pouring onto surface and hand); Image 2 = flammable (flame); Image 3 = toxic (skull and crossbones). Labelled Image 1, Image 2, Image 3.

topic: Limestone reaction results (Trial 1 data table)
image_prompt: SVG data table 'Trial 1': columns Time / s, Volume of CO₂ produced from limestone samples / cm³ (Trial 1, Trial 2, Trial 3), Average volume of gas produced / cm³ (Average). Rows by time 0..70: (0,0,0,0,0),(10,17,16,15,16),(20,33,30,31,32),(30,46,48,44,46),(40,57,67,55,BLANK),(50,70,70,70,70),(60,81,83,82,82),(70,93,91,92,92). The 40 s Average cell is left blank for the student.

topic: Fractional distillation column
Hydrocarbon chain length MCQ (A/B/C)
image_prompt: SVG: three ball-and-stick hydrocarbon molecules labelled A, B, C. A = methane (1 carbon, 4 hydrogens); B = medium chain (~8 carbons); C = long chain (~12 carbons). Carbon = dark grey larger spheres, Hydrogen = white small spheres, with a Key. Answer A (shortest) is distilled with petroleum gases.

topic: Oil spill cleanup methods
image_prompt: SVG sea scene showing three oil-spill cleanup methods: (1) Oil collected from surface water — inflated boom corrals slick towards a skimmer ship; (2) Burning — oil contained by a fire-retardant boom and set alight; (3) Dispersion — aeroplane sprays chemicals that break down the oil. Labels for each method.

topic: Country X map (crude oil transport)
image_prompt: SVG stylised map of Country X: two islands (North Island with an oil field, South Island with the capital) in a blue sea, connected by a bridge and ferry. Markers for Refinery, Port, Capital (star), Oil field, plus roads and railway lines, a fishing zone, coral reefs and a protected area. Compass rose and a legend.
